Title: Heterologous expression of Bixa orellana cleavage dioxygenase 4–3 drives crocin but not bixin biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Color/nutraceutical compound; glycosylation increases water solubility and bioavailability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: In combination with TP:BoCCD4-3, promotes accumulation of highly glycosylated crocins in tomato fruits
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transgenic expression in tomato; LC–PDA–HRMS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CsUGT74AD1 from Crocus sativus was co-expressed with TP:BoCCD4-3 in tomato fruits. 2. It specifically catalyzes crocetin glycosylation. 3. Its co-expression contributed to high crocin accumulation and a saffron-like profile enriched in highly glycosylated crocins.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Bixa transcriptome re-analysis; NGS expression data SRX1117606 and ERX2099513 (study-level, not gene-specific)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful partner gene for crocin engineering to increase glycosylation state and saffron-like crocin profile in crop tissues
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in this paper as a saffron UGT mediating crocetin glycosylation based on prior work and used here for metabolic engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Escherichia coli; Nicotiana benthamiana; Tobacco; Tomato
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Escherichia coli; Nicotiana benthamiana; Nicotiana tabacum; Solanum lycopersicum
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: N. tabacum cv. Petit Havana; tomato cv. Microtom
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Arabinose-inducible bacterial expression; CaMV 35S (constitutive) in N. benthamiana and tomato; T7 promoter with theophylline-inducible synthetic riboswitch system in tobacco plastids
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Crocetin dialdehyde-forming cleavage step of carotenoid-derived crocin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/apocarotenoid-generating cleavage step upstream of crocetin and crocin formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleave lycopene, β-carotene, and zeaxanthin mainly at the 7,8/7′,8′ positions to generate the C20 apocarotenal crocetin dialdehyde; can also yield low levels of single-cleavage intermediates and citraurin-related product in planta
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control cleavage step enabling crocin production in heterologous tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcriptome-derived CCD identified in Bixa; detailed native expression not established here
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed in leaves, plastids, and fruits
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves; N. tabacum leaves; tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives high crocin accumulation but not bixin biosynthesis; in tomato fruits crocins exceeded 100 μg/g DW
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in carotenoid-producing E. coli; LC–PDA–HRMS; authentic crocetin dialdehyde standard co-migration and MS/MS; transient expression in N. benthamiana; plastid transformation in tobacco; stable transgenic expression in tomato
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabinose induction in E. coli; transient leaf expression; theophylline-inducible transplastomic system in tobacco with leakiness; ripe tomato fruits
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Bixa transcriptome re-analysis; NGS data SRX1117606 and ERX2099513 (study-level)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of crocins across tissues; especially promising in tomato fruit, potentially combined with glycosyltransferases, lycopene-boosting alleles, storage traits, and fruit-specific promoters
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Leaf bleaching/pale phenotype in transplastomic tobacco; inability to survive on soil due to carotenoid depletion; adverse leaf phenotypes in highest-expressing tomato lines
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BoCCD4-3 was the only tested Bixa CCD that robustly produced crocetin dialdehyde from lycopene, β-carotene, and zeaxanthin in E. coli. 2. It did not specifically produce bixin dialdehyde, arguing against its proposed role in bixin biosynthesis. 3. BoCCD4-3 localizes to plastids despite lacking a recognizable transit peptide. 4. Its expression in Nicotiana leaves, tobacco plastids, and tomato fruits drove crocetin/crocin accumulation. 5. Co-expression with CsUGT74AD1 in tomato yielded the highest crocin levels and a saffron-like highly glycosylated crocin profile.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Broader substrate specificity than CsCCD2; also associated with trace intermediate apocarotenoids and citraurin in transformed tomato fruits.
